Reinforcement Learning Problem
RL systems are defined by a tuple of elements
{
X
,
U
,
T
,
R
,
ρ
0
}
{X,U,T,R,ρ 
0
​
 }. Here 
X
X stands for the set of possible agent states i.e. 
x
∈
X
x∈X, for instance the position and velocity of the child and bike in the above example. 
u
∈
U
u∈U is the action taken to steer the evolution of the state, e.g., the forces exerted on the bike pedals. Numerically 
x
x and 
u
u are usually vectors whose entries include quantities that the agent can acquire and implement.
